question.text: An ATM NIFTY call option has theta = -15 and gamma = 0.002. If 5 days pass with no movement in NIFTY, what is the approximate premium loss from time decay?

answer.text: Rs 75 per unit (15 × 5 = 75), which is Rs 1,875 per lot (75 × 25)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Theta = -15 means the option loses Rs 15 per day. Over 5 days (assuming constant theta): 15 × 5 = Rs 75 per unit. Per lot (25): 75 × 25 = Rs 1,875. Note: theta actually accelerates near expiry, so this is an approximation. **The Trap:** Rs 15 is for ONE day only. Rs 375 multiplies incorrectly. Even with no underlying movement, the option LOSES value from time decay — 'no movement' doesn't mean 'no change in premium'. **Cross-Exam Context:** Theta impact calculations are the same across all option markets. This demonstrates why options are called 'wasting assets'. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Rs 1,875 per lot over 5 days is the cost of holding this option — multiply by your number of lots to understand your daily 'carrying cost'. This helps decide whether to hold or cut a position.
